key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration classification reliably between fundamentally different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent monitoring across new welding systems and improves practical robustness under process-to-process domain shift.
method: Train a UDA model to align feature distributions between source and target welding domains.
Use gradual source domain expansion to progressively broaden the source training set and stabilize adaptation.
Learn domain-invariant yet class-discriminative representations for penetration status prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, all well above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance is validated on dedicated datasets, so real shop-floor deployment may still require checking robustness to sensor noise, parameter variation, and unseen joint/material conditions.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
